01 — Profile

About Me

I'm an Aerospace Engineering sophomore at NC State University, driven by a passion for flight mechanics, structural design, and the science of pushing vehicles beyond the limits of conventional engineering.

My hands-on experience spans composite fabrication, CAD modeling in SolidWorks, aerodynamic simulation, and engineering analysis with MATLAB — skills I've applied in both academic and competition settings.

Currently competing in the NASA Student Launch initiative as part of the NCSU High Powered Rocketry Club, where I contribute to the Structures Team handling airframe integration and composite fin fabrication.

Professional Summary

Aerospace Engineering student with a strong foundation in aerodynamics, solid mechanics, and rocketry. Experienced in high-impact projects including a NASA Student Launch competition entry, focusing on structural assembly and composite fabrication. Eager to leverage hands-on technical abilities in SolidWorks, MATLAB, and OpenRocket to contribute to innovative design solutions and achieve mission-critical outcomes in a dynamic aerospace co-op environment.

Education
North Carolina State University — Raleigh, NC
B.S., Aerospace Engineering  ·  GPA: 3.2  ·  Dean's List: Fall 2024
Coursework: Applied Differential Equations, Intro to MATLAB, Foundation of Graphics - SolidWorks, Aerospace Vehicular Performance, Intro to Aerospace Engineering, Aerodynamics I, Solid Mechanics, Experimental Aerodynamics I
Expected May 2028
Projects
High Powered Rocketry Club | NC State University
Aug 2025 – Present
  • ›Contributed to the Structures Team in assembly and integration of a full-scale high-powered rocket for NASA Student Launch.
  • ›Fabricated high-strength composite fins via carbon fiber & fiberglass layup with epoxy resin using vacuum bagging techniques.
  • ›Utilized power tools including Dremel and belt sander to precisely shape and finish rocket body sections.
  • ›Assembled critical airframe sections including bulkheads designed to withstand high-G launch forces.
Automated Chicken Coop Door | NC State University
Jan 2025 – Apr 2025
  • ›Engineered a 10×12 inch automated door using a high-torque motor and pulley system, resolving torque deficiencies during prototyping.
  • ›Led a 3-member team ensuring all milestones were completed and submitted on time.
  • ›Constructed durable assembly using sheet metal door and balsa wood frame with automated limit switch cutoff.
KidWind Open Division | NC School of Science and Mathematics
Aug 2023 – Apr 2024
  • ›Designed and built a 5-blade windmill using a 3V DC motor, achieving 4.1 joules output measured via multimeter.
  • ›Optimized blade pitch through iterative wind tunnel testing and presented findings to a panel of judges.
  • ›Managed budget tracking in Excel and created comprehensive PowerPoint presentations for each development stage.
